title: Eusteralis sampsonii
intro: Eusteralis sampsonii, also known as Sampson's snakeroot, is a type of flowering plant in the carrot family, Apiaceae. The plant has been traditionally used in herbal medicine to treat various health conditions, including fever, rheumatism, and respiratory issues, due to its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The bioactive constituents of Eusteralis sampsonii include sesquiterpenes, flavonoids, and alkaloids, which contribute to its medicinal properties. Eusteralis sampsonii is often used to make herbal preparations such as teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are commonly used to treat colds, sore throats, and other respiratory ailments.
